original_hypothesis.title: BlastOnboardingBoot ready Flag Can Be Toggled to Change Staking Contract After Users Claim
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setReady() allows the owner to set ready to true or false. setStaking() only blocks changes when ready==true. Owner can: (1) setReady(true), (2) users claim and stake to staking contract A, (3) setReady(false), (4) setStaking(B) - change to malicious contract, (5) setReady(true), (6) remaining users claim and stake to contract B. Early claimers are in contract A, late claimers in B. If B is malicious, late claimers lose funds. Early claimers may lose rewards if reward configuration targets only B.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The ready flag is not one-way. The guard on setStaking only checks current ready state, not whether any claims have been processed.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setReady accepts bool _ready with no irreversibility check; setStaking at line 137 only checks `if (ready) revert`

preconditions: Owner has control of BlastOnboarding proxy
Some users have claimed, others have not

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Owner toggles ready false, changes staking contract, toggles ready true again
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Between first claim and last claim

operational_questions: Is the owner a multisig with sufficient operational security?
Has setReady been called multiple times in any deployment?
Are there time delays or governance requirements on owner actions?
validation_requirements: Verify setReady(false) can be called after setReady(true)
Verify setStaking can be called after toggling ready to false

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Owner sets ready=true, some users claim and stake to legitimate staking contract
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Owner sets ready=false, calls setStaking with malicious contract
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Remaining users claim and stake to malicious contract, losing their LP shares
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Owner key compromise or malicious owner

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Late-claiming users' LP shares directed to malicious staking contract
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Split staking between two contracts breaks reward distribution
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Cannot recover LP shares staked to malicious contract
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All claim operations after staking contract change

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Between first and last user claims

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Multiple setReady calls or setStaking calls

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
